text.title: rag-eval-strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rag-eval-strategy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Single Metric Trap — relying on one metric (e.g., only faithfulness or only Hit Rate) as the sole quality signal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No single metric fully characterizes RAG quality. Faithfulness can be high while the retriever returns irrelevant context. Hit Rate can be 1.0 while Precision@5 is 0.20. Always use a combination covering both retrieval and generation dimensions.

failure-mode.description: No Baseline — measuring metric values without a stored baseline to compare against.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An absolute score of 0.75 is meaningless without knowing whether that is better or worse than the previous version. Store baseline metric values at every release and compare explicitly. A 5-point regression is actionable even if 0.75 seems "acceptable".

failure-mode.description: Non-representative Test Set — using a convenience sample that doesn't reflect production query distribution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Metrics computed on easy or in-distribution queries overestimate real-world quality. Include difficult queries (ambiguous, multi-hop, out-of-domain) and edge cases in the test set. Update the test set as production queries evolve.

failure-mode.description: Static Ground Truth — never updating the ground-truth relevant doc IDs and expected answers as the document corpus evolves.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ground truth becomes stale as new documents are added, old ones removed, or expected answer wording changes. A retrieval metric computed against outdated ground truth is measuring the wrong thing. Schedule ground-truth reviews with corpus changes.

failure-mode.description: Evaluator Bias — treating LLM-judge scores as ground truth without acknowledging the evaluator's own biases and inconsistencies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM evaluators have systematic biases: preference for verbosity, self-preference (evaluating own outputs higher), and position bias (scoring first item in a list higher). Calibrate LLM-judge scores against human labels on a sample before relying on them as quality gates.
